ffet_definition: one FFET maps to exactly one seed/sub-surface unless explicit merge_rationale proves inseparability

ffets.objective: Implement the 6B product record authority fine-grained execution surface for Product Catalogue Service.
scope: Create the seed-specific contract/control artifact for 6B product record authority.
Create the seed-specific Phase 6B API service or primitive artifact for 6B product record authority.
Create the seed-specific test that proves the accepted contract or service behavior for 6B product record authority.
Keep implementation within the exact files for this FFET.
non_scope: No pricing engine, payment allocation math, provider callback processing, communication sending, reconciliation behavior, frontend screen, migration, credential handling, production adapter behavior, or irreversible customer action unless this FFET explicitly names it in scope.
No edits to shared Phase 6B module/controller/service scaffold files unless a separate approved integration FFET owns that exact file.
No new architecture, schema, permission, API, ADL, service-boundary, or business decision.

source_authority_chain.title: Owned data: Products, categories, product media, product status, product history; activation/lifecycle: Foundry-managed service; archive over delete; billing/evidence: Product record evidence; optional per-record pricing

files_forbidden_to_change: docs/process/v4_1/phase_6b/** except completion evidence explicitly authorized by this FFET
shared Phase 6B scaffold files not listed in files_expected_to_change
required_outputs: Seed-specific contract/control artifact compiles under the contracts validation command.
Seed-specific API service or primitive artifact compiles under API typecheck.
Seed-specific test exercises the accepted behavior or control surface for this seed.
evidence_artifacts: validation command output captured in PR summary
expected_validation_evidence: `pnpm --filter @akti/api test` passes with the seed-specific test included.
acceptance_criteria: All files in files_expected_to_change exist after implementation.
No file outside files_expected_to_change is modified except explicitly allowed completion evidence.
The seed-specific test fails before the behavior/control artifact is implemented and passes after implementation.
All validation_commands pass.
No ticket-generation or execution flag is changed by implementation.
ffets.runtime_behavior_to_implement: Seed-specific capability surface ffet behavior for 6B product record authority is represented by a typed contract/control artifact and a passing API-side test under the accepted Phase 6B scaffold boundary.
ffets.maximum_concrete_capability_statement: Apply maximum concrete capability within this one-seed FFET scope without adding adjacent seed behavior or shared scaffold integration ownership.
ffets.minimum_concrete_requirement: The seed-specific test for 6B product record authority passes under `pnpm --filter @akti/api test` and proves the contract/control behavior implemented in files_expected_to_change.
stop_conditions: Stop if any required file must move outside files_expected_to_change.
Stop if implementation requires a new schema field, API route shape, permission, ADL interpretation, event, service boundary, provider behavior, or business rule not already committed.
Stop if a shared module/controller/service file must be edited and is not in files_expected_to_change.
Stop if validation fails after 3 bounded self-heal attempts.
Stop if an irreversible customer-impacting action would be introduced.
ffets.rollback_notes: Revert only the files_expected_to_change for this FFET; do not revert unrelated Phase 6B scaffold/control files or other FFETs.
requires_human_approval_if: A source decision is missing or contradictory.
A schema/API/permission/event/service-boundary choice is needed.
The exact file plan must expand beyond this FFET.
An irreversible action, destructive migration, credential, or production provider behavior is introduced.
self_audit_checks: Verify files_expected_to_change are the only modified implementation files.
Verify no forbidden file changed.
Verify validation_commands pass.
Verify no hidden blocker, placeholder, broad glob, or documentation-only MCR is introduced.

allowed: deterministic TypeScript import/export mismatch inside files_expected_to_change
deterministic Zod/schema shape mismatch inside files_expected_to_change
seed-specific test expectation mismatch caused by active FFET files
forbidden: invent schema fields, APIs, permissions, events, ADLs, service boundaries, or business rules

promotion_criteria: Independent FFET audit passes.
Binding human approval flips ticket_generation_allowed and execution_authorized for this FFET or approved batch.
Dependencies are completed or accepted on main.
Freshness check against current repo HEAD passes immediately before execution.
